See Jennifer Tappan, [Medicalizing[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0516.01} Malnutrition: Nearly a Century of Kwashiorkor Work in a Small Part of Africa]{.ePub-I} (Athens: Ohio University Press, forthcoming).

[Conclusion: Responding to Ebola]{.ePub-BI}

[1](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-1){#notes.xhtml_bm1-1}. Saliou Samb, "WHO Says Guinea Ebola Outbreak Small as MSF Slams International Response," Reuters, April 1, 2014.

[2](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-2){#notes.xhtml_bm1-2}. Ibid.

[3](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-3){#notes.xhtml_bm1-3}. "Ebola Response: Where Are We Now?" Médicins Sans Frontières / Doctors Without Borders, [www.doctorswithoutborders.org/document/ebola-response-where-are-we-now/](http://www.doctorswithoutborders.org/document/ebola-response-where-are-we-now/)[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0516.02}.

[4](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-4){#notes.xhtml_bm1-4}. Norimitsu Onishi, "Empty Ebola Clinics in Liberia Are Seen as Misstep in U.S. Relief Effort," [New York Times]{.ePub-I}, April 11, 2015.

[5](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-5){#notes.xhtml_bm1-5}. Nelson Dunbar, Director of Research, Ministry of Health and Social Welfare, Republic of Liberia, "Ebola: In the Trenches; Perspectives from Liberia about the Ebola Response," presented at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, May 18, 2015.

[6](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-6){#notes.xhtml_bm1-6}. Norimitsu[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0516.03} Onishi, "As Ebola Ebbs in Africa, Focus Turns from Death to Life," [New York Times]{.ePub-I}, January 31, 2015.

[7](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-7){#notes.xhtml_bm1-7}. "The Ebola Hot Zone," [60 Minutes]{.ePub-I}, November 9, 2014.

[8](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-8){#notes.xhtml_bm1-8}. Peter Redfield, [Life in Crisis: The Ethical Journey of Doctors Without Borders]{.ePub-I} (Berkeley: University of California Press, 2013).

[9](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-9){#notes.xhtml_bm1-9}. Ibid., 177.

[10](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-10){#notes.xhtml_bm1-10}. MSF's strategy to control Ebola has been organized into six elements: (1) isolation and[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0516.04} supportive medical care for cases, including laboratory capacity to confirm an infection; (2) safe-burial activities in case-management facilities and communities; (3) awareness-raising; (4) alerts and surveillance in the community; (5) contact tracing; and (6) access to health care for non-Ebola patients, including the protection of health facilities and health workers. MSF also started distributing[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0516.05} antimalarial medicine in Monrovia, where the health []{#notes.xhtml_pg_393}system had collapsed, leaving residents of the city without access to these drugs. All of their work was carried out by their own staff, who had limited engagement with local health authorities.

[11](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-11){#notes.xhtml_bm1-11}. Paul Farmer, [AIDS and Accusation: Haiti and the Geography of Blame]{.ePub-I} (Berkeley: University of California Press, 1992).

[12](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-12){#notes.xhtml_bm1-12}. People commenting on the[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0517.01} PIH website complained about this delay and wondered why they should be donating to PIH.

[13](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-13){#notes.xhtml_bm1-13}. Tomas Kellner, "Coalition Fights Ebola at the Outbreak's Invisible Frontline in Remote African Rainforest," GE Reports, October 14, 2014, [www.gereports.com/post/98748927415/coalition-fights-ebola-at-the-outbreaks-invisible/](http://www.gereports.com/post/98748927415/coalition-fights-ebola-at-the-outbreaks-invisible/).

[14](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-14){#notes.xhtml_bm1-14}. "Why Ebola Costs Could Top \$4bln in 2015," Times Video, December 29, 2014,[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0517.02} [www.nytimes.com/video/multimedia/100000003421592/why-ebola-costs-could-top-4bln-in-2015.html](http://www.nytimes.com/video/multimedia/100000003421592/why-ebola-costs-could-top-4bln-in-2015.html).

[15](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-15){#notes.xhtml_bm1-15}. "Integrated Delivery: Strategy Overview," Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, [www.gatesfoundation.org/What-We-Do/Global-Development/Integrated-Delivery/](http://www.gatesfoundation.org/What-We-Do/Global-Development/Integrated-Delivery/).

[16](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-16){#notes.xhtml_bm1-16}. Prabhjot Singh and Jeffrey Sachs, "1 Million Community Health Workers in Sub-Saharan Africa," [Lancet]{.ePub-I} 383, 9889 (2013): 285.

[17](#conclusion.xhtml_bm1-17){#notes.xhtml_bm1-17}. "Economists[]{#notes.xhtml_GBS.0517.03} Declaration," Global Coalition for Universal Health Coverage, <http://universalhealthcoverageday.org/economists-declaration/>.
